paper_title: Impact of managerial human resource quality on growth of firms in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam

entities.id: Impact of managerial human resource quality on growth of firms in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam
entities.description: The main research topic investigating how manager quality influences business expansion.
Primary author of the research paper.

entities.id: College of Economics, Can Tho University
entities.description: The academic organization the authors are affiliated with in Vietnam.
The primary field of research for this study.
A composite concept including professional knowledge, experience, and skills of managers.
The dependent variable measured by the annual growth rate of sales.
Theoretical framework suggesting that organizational outcomes are reflections of the values and cognitive bases of powerful actors.
Statistical method used to estimate the parameters of the linear regression model.
Data collection instrument used to interview top managers.
Numerical data collected from firms randomly selected in the Mekong Delta.
Data retrieved from sources like the General Statistical Office of Vietnam.

entities.id: Growth rate of medium and small-sized firms (17.2% per year)
entities.description: Specific annual growth percentage for SMEs in the survey.

entities.id: Growth rate for large firms (26.5% per year)

Multicollinearity diagnostic value indicating model reliability.

Statistically significant impact value of exports on firm growth.
